seo.title: Databases for AI Apps implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Databases for AI Apps projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a technical roadmap for preparing database infrastructure to support production-grade AI applications, focusing on vector performance, connection stability in serverless environments, and data integrity for RAG workflows.

items.title: Index Dimension Alignment
items.description: Verify that the vector column dimensions (e.g., 1536 for OpenAI) exactly match the output of the embedding model to prevent runtime query failures.

items.title: HNSW/IVFFlat Indexing
items.description: Apply HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) or IVFFlat indexes to embedding columns; avoid sequential scans for datasets exceeding 10,000 rows.

items.title: Distance Metric Consistency
items.description: Confirm the database distance function (cosine, L2, or inner product) matches the metric the embedding model was trained on.

items.title: Memory-to-Index Ratio
items.description: Ensure the database instance RAM is large enough to hold the entire vector index (e.g., HNSW) to avoid disk swapping during similarity searches.

items.title: Index Build Parameters
items.description: Adjust m and ef_construction parameters for HNSW to balance build time versus search recall based on your specific latency requirements.

items.title: External Connection Pooling
items.description: Deploy PgBouncer or use a platform-native pooler (e.g., Supabase, Neon) to manage connection overhead from serverless functions.

items.title: Statement Timeouts
items.description: Set a strict statement_timeout (e.g., 30s) to prevent long-running AI queries or vector index builds from exhausting the connection pool.

items.title: Prisma/Drizzle Transaction Mode
items.description: Configure ORM connection strings for transaction mode if using a pooler to prevent prepared statement errors in serverless environments.

items.title: Edge Runtime Compatibility
items.description: Verify that database drivers use HTTP or WebSocket protocols if deploying to edge runtimes like Cloudflare Workers or Vercel Edge.

items.title: Idle Connection Pruning
items.description: Configure the pooler to close idle connections after 60 seconds to accommodate the bursty nature of LLM application traffic.

items.title: Embedding Versioning
items.description: Include a 'model_version' column in tables storing vectors to facilitate re-indexing if you migrate from one embedding model to another.

items.title: Metadata Indexing
items.description: Apply GIN indexes to JSONB columns used for filtering vector results (e.g., user_id, organization_id) to ensure hybrid search performance.

items.title: Conversation TTLs
items.description: Implement a partitioning or cleanup strategy for chat history tables to prevent unbounded storage growth and query degradation.

items.title: Source Link Integrity
items.description: Enforce foreign key constraints between embedding chunks and their source documents to prevent orphaned vectors in RAG systems.

items.title: Audit Logging
items.description: Enable row-level logging for tables containing sensitive context data used in AI prompts to meet compliance requirements.

items.title: Parallel Worker Configuration
items.description: Increase max_parallel_workers_per_gather to allow the database to use multiple CPU cores for large-scale vector similarity scans.

items.title: Batch Insert Strategy
items.description: Implement batching for vector inserts (e.g., 100-500 rows per transaction) to minimize WAL (Write-Ahead Log) overhead during data ingestion.

items.title: Read Replica Offloading
items.description: Route heavy RAG retrieval queries to read replicas to ensure embedding generation and chat history writes remain performant on the primary.

items.title: Query Plan Validation
items.description: Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on common vector queries to confirm that indexes are being utilized rather than full table scans.

items.title: Vector Quantization
items.description: Test scalar or product quantization if vector storage costs exceed budget or if index size exceeds available RAM.

items.title: Row Level Security (RLS)
items.description: Enable and test RLS policies to ensure that vector similarity searches only return data the requesting user is authorized to view.

items.title: PII Scrubbing
items.description: Validate that the ingestion pipeline scrubs PII from text before it is stored in the database or sent to embedding providers.

items.title: VPC Peering
items.description: Establish VPC peering or PrivateLink between the application backend and the database to prevent exposure of sensitive AI data to the public internet.

items.title: Encryption at Rest
items.description: Verify that the database provider has AES-256 encryption enabled for all storage volumes and automated backups.

items.title: Least Privilege Roles
items.description: Create a dedicated database user for the AI service with permissions restricted to specific schemas and tables.
